opinion.h1: VAN HOOK v. UNITED STATES, 365 U.S. 609 (1961)
opinion.h4: VAN HOOK v. UNITED STATES. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SEVENTH CIRCUIT. No. 705. Decided March 27, 1961.
opinion.p: Certiorari granted; judgment reversed; and case remanded.
Solicitor General Cox, Assistant Attorney General Miller and Beatrice Rosenberg for the United States.

The petition for writ of certiorari is granted. The judgment is reversed and the case remanded for resentencing in compliance with Rule 32 of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure. Green v. United States, 365 U.S. 301.
365 U.S. 609 HITCHCOCK v. ARIZONA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ARIZONA. No. 718, Misc. Decided March 27, 1961.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
